sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) dust, dry earth (trockene Erde), Gen 2:7 26:15 Jos 7:6 Job 2:12 also used of clay or loam, of which walls are made, Lev 14:42, 45 Lev 14:45of a heap of rubbish (Schutt), Habak. 1:10 very rarely of fine dust, such as is blown by the wind, i.q. אָבָק Psa 18:43. עַל עָפָר-(a) in the earth, in the world, Job 19:25 39:14 41:25 also upon the ground, Job 22:24 Isa 47:1 (b) in the grave, Job 20:11 21:26 for which there is also said לֶעָפָר Job 7:21. יָרַד עָפָר to go down to the dust, i.e. into the grave, Psa 22:30 30:10 . שׁוּב אֶל־עָפָר to return to dust, Gen 3:19 Psa 104:29. עָפָר וָאֵפֶר dust and ashes, a proverbial phrase to express the lowness and fragility of human nature, Gen 18:27 Psa 103:14. It is used of multitude, Num 23:10, עֲפַר יַעֲקֹב “the dust of Jacob,” i.e. Jacob, who is as numerous as the dust of earth, compare חֹול. אָכַל עָפָר to eat dust, used of the serpent, Gen 3:14 compare Isa 65:25 but figuratively used, Lam 3:29, “to put the mouth in the dust,” i.e. to be silent and wait the aid of God.-Plur. עֲפָרֹות clods of earth. Pro 8:26, רֹאשׁ עַפְרֹות תֵּבֵל “the first of the clods of the world.” Job 28:6, עַפְרֹות זָהָב lumps of gold in mines.
